hookCapture.patchTarget: Parse-postfix (OK: ArgumentExtensions.Parse, OK: CommandExtensions.Parse, OK: CommandExtensions.Parse, OK: OptionExtensions.Parse, OK: Parser.Parse, OK: ParserExtensions.Parse, OK: CommandExtensions.Invoke, OK: CommandExtensions.Invoke, OK: ParseResultExtensions.Invoke, OK: ParserExtensions.Invoke, OK: ParserExtensions.Invoke, OK: CommandExtensions.InvokeAsync, OK: CommandExtensions.InvokeAsync, OK: ParseResultExtensions.InvokeAsync, OK: ParserExtensions.InvokeAsync, OK: ParserExtensions.InvokeAsync, OK: RootCommand.ctor(String))

options.name: inputFiles
options.description: The relative or absolute path to the input files

options.name: inputDirectory
options.description: The relative or absolute path to the input directory

options.name: outputDirectory
options.description: The relative or absolute path to the output directory

options.name: outputFiles
options.description: The relative or absolute path to the output files

options.name: pattern
options.description: The search string to match against the names of files in the input directory [default: System.String[]]

options.name: recursive
options.description: Recurse into subdirectories of input directory search [default: True]

options.name: extension
options.description: The output file extension [default: txt]

options.name: settingsFile
options.description: The relative or absolute path to the settings file

options.name: temperature
options.description: What sampling temperature to use, between 0 and 2. Higher values like 0.8 will make the output more random, while lower values like 0.2 will make it more focused and deterministic. [default: 0.7]

options.name: topP
options.description: An alternative to sampling with temperature, called nucleus sampling, where the model considers the results of the tokens with top_p probability mass. So 0.1 means only the tokens comprising the top 10% probability mass are considered. [default: 0.7]

options.name: presencePenalty
options.description: Number between -2.0 and 2.0. Positive values penalize new tokens based on whether they appear in the text so far, increasing the model's likelihood to talk about new topics. [default: 0.7]

options.name: frequencyPenalty
options.description: Number between -2.0 and 2.0. Positive values penalize new tokens based on their existing frequency in the text so far, decreasing the model's likelihood to repeat the same line verbatim. [default: 0.7]

options.name: maxTokens
options.description: The maximum number of tokens to generate in the chat completion. [default: 2000]

options.name: apiKey
options.description: Override OpenAI api key. By default OPENAI_API_KEY environment variable is used.

options.name: apiUrl
options.description: Override OpenAI api url. By default OPENAI_API_URL_CHAT_COMPLETIONS environment variable is used.

options.name: model
options.description: ID of the model to use. See the model endpoint compatibility table for details on which models work with the Chat API. [default: gpt-3.5-turbo]

options.name: directions
options.description: The system message (directions) helps set the behavior of the assistant. Typically, a conversation is formatted with a system message first, followed by alternating user and assistant messages. [default: You are a helpful assistant.]

options.name: threads
options.description: The number of parallel job threads [default: 1]

options.name: quiet
options.description: Set verbosity level to quiet

options.name: version
options.description: Show version information

options.name: help
options.description: Show help and usage information
